title: Positivity/analyticity bootstrap for pion GFFs
research_question: What is the strongest model-independent bound on pion gravitational form factors (GFFs) from positivity/analyticity/dispersive constraints without coupled channels?
strategy_notes: Start from a single-channel dispersive representation for pion GFFs and implement positivity constraints on spectral densities / moments. Avoid coupled-channel inputs by construction; treat subtraction constants/normalizations as parameters with explicit priors/constraints.

evaluation_metrics.description: Quantify tightening vs. baseline literature bounds at fixed kinematic points (e.g., t=0 or small |t|).
evaluation_metrics.target: At least one numerically reproducible bound stronger or qualitatively different than the seed papers.

next_actions: Extract normalizations/definitions and the exact positivity inequality assumptions from seed papers (LaTeX-first).
Specify an explicit optimization variable set and constraint set (laptop-feasible).
Add a numerics validation plan (toy checks + regression outputs).

key_artifacts.description: Per-island plan (this artifact) used by validate-project to enforce required structure.
